title: [Bug]: PR label sync should remove stale managed labels
summary: The pull request label synchronization helper adds source issue labels but does not remove stale managed labels copied from earlier or accidental source issues.
observed: After a source issue detection mistake, the PR kept labels from the wrong issue until they were removed manually.
expected: Label synchronization should remove known managed labels that are no longer present in the current source issue label union while preserving unknown manual labels.
reproduction: Run label synchronization once with an accidental source issue and then again with the correct source issue only.
failing_test: Add a unit test proving stale managed labels are removed while unknown manual labels are preserved.
impact: Pull requests can appear in the wrong release, sink, security, or testing filters. Runtime package behavior is unaffected.
delivery_semantics: This bug is limited to GitHub workflow metadata automation and cannot affect commit-then-ACK behavior.
security: The fix must remove only known project-managed labels and must not remove unrelated maintainer labels accidentally.
acceptance: Known managed labels not present in the source issue label union are removed from the PR.
The helper supports an opt-out for stale-label removal if needed.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_pr_label_sync.py tests/unit/test_pr_auto_approval.py -q and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update workflow documentation to mention stale managed label cleanup.
closeout: Close after the bug branch merges into the release branch and release-gated closure confirms the release has shipped.
